# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1  # version of Istio API that the resource will be deployed against (latest at time of writing)
kind: PeerAuthenticationPolicy   # defines a policy for peer authentication with other services, similar to Gateway rules in V3 MeshRules but used instead by ServiceMeshes. For example when you want an mTLS connection across all the microservices or pods within your namespace 
metadata:                            # contains metadata of rule (can be omitted if no labels are specified)  
    name: peer-authentication         # unique resource identity, will override 'name' field from spec which is required when created programmatically by clients. Required for read/write APIs like ListPeerAuthenticationPolicy and CreatePeerAuthenticatedService 
spec:                               # details to define the behavior of policy  
    mtlsMode: PERMISSIVE               # one or more options available, including STRICT (default), ALLOWLIST & ENFORCING. In order for Istio Sidecar Envoy Proxy not start handshake if any AuthenticatedPeer's certificate is invalid; instead it will return error
    selector:                         # selectors that the rule applies to  - here all microservices within 'default namespace'. Can also be namespaced like default.yournamespace, where you specify your-microservice name (if different)  
      matchLabels:                     # label key/value pairs for service selection using labels and SelectorSpans from baggage query parameters in request headers  - can include multiple keys per a resource such as the environment ('myapp') or version of software components. Can also be namespaced if 'yournamespace' is specified
        app: your-microservice           # matchLabels based on key/value pairs present within label selectors, used for selecting Istio InboundGateway and DestinationRule resources  - replace with the name or number of specific pod(s) in a namespace. The 'app' field is case insensitive
